proposed_hypotheses.text: The marginal probability of objective_response in the cohort differs from 0.5 (i.e., is not balanced).

analyses.result_summary: Overall response rate is 0.1875 (9375 of 50000); a binomial test against 0.5 gives p < 1e-300, confirming objective_response is the minority outcome.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 16 binary feature columns, at least one is associated with objective_response (chi-square test) at p<1e-6.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: chi2_contingency(crosstab(df[c], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Three binary features show very strong positive association with response: feature_014 (p_resp 0.235 vs 0.167, chi2 p=5.8e-70), feature_001 (0.221 vs 0.165, p=1.9e-55), feature_021 (0.220 vs 0.166, p=6.6e-51). Two binary features show weaker negative associations: feature_026 (0.168 vs 0.191, p=3.3e-06) and feature_009 (0.166 vs 0.190, p=3.5e-05). The remaining 11 binary features show no detectable association (p>0.1).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_004 (3-level, integer 0/1/2) is monotonically and negatively associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate falls monotonically with feature_004: level 0 = 0.231 (n=17592), level 1 = 0.174 (n=24971), level 2 = 0.129 (n=7437). Ordinal logistic coefficient = -0.379 (z = -19.9, p ~ 3e-88). Pattern is consistent with a performance-status-like variable where higher values mean worse condition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 19 continuous/ordinal features, at least one differs in mean between responders and non-responders.
feature_011 (a continuous variable centered around 68 with range 0-92, plausibly age in years) is associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: for c in cont_cols: ttest_ind(df.loc[resp==0,c], df.loc[resp==1,c])
analyses.result_summary: Multiple continuous features differ between responders and non-responders. Strongest signals (lower in responders): feature_024 (60.30 vs 58.50, p=2.3e-16), feature_020 (3.95 vs 3.44, p=8.0e-31), feature_027 (6.17 vs 5.69, p=6.1e-06), feature_022 (20.39 vs 19.27, p=1.8e-04). Higher in responders: feature_029 (3.795 vs 3.824, p=2.8e-07).
feature_011 (likely age) shows essentially no marginal association with response: mean 67.99 in non-responders vs 67.98 in responders, t-test p=0.88.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression of objective_response on all features simultaneously, the same handful of features identified marginally (feature_001, feature_014, feature_021, feature_004, feature_026, feature_009, feature_024, feature_020, feature_029, feature_022, feature_027) remain independently associated with the outcome.

analyses.result_summary: Joint logistic regression confirms the same set: feature_014 (beta +0.436, p=4e-71), feature_001 (+0.374, p=2e-57), feature_021 (+0.362, p=5e-54), feature_004 (-0.362, p=1e-92), feature_020 (-0.037, p=2e-31), feature_024 (-0.005, p=2e-17), feature_029 (+0.122, p=1.4e-7), feature_026 (-0.169, p=6.1e-7), feature_009 (-0.188, p=3.6e-6), feature_027 (-0.0065, p=6e-6), feature_022 (-0.0018, p=1.3e-4). Most other features (including feature_011/age and demographic-looking binaries) are not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001, feature_014, and feature_021 behave like randomized treatment indicators: they are mutually independent and independent of all other features.

analyses.code: for tx in [001,014,021]: chi2_contingency vs every binary; ttest vs every continuous
analyses.result_summary: For each of feature_001, feature_014, feature_021, association with every other feature in the dataset has p > 1e-6 (no detectable confounding). Pairwise crosstabs are consistent with independent assignment (e.g., P(feature_001=1 | feature_014=1) ~ 0.40 = unconditional rate). Strongly consistent with three independent randomized treatment arms.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the three putative treatments (feature_001, feature_014, feature_021) increases objective_response on its own (positive marginal main effect).

analyses.code: Compare response in tx=1 vs tx=0 for each treatment
analyses.result_summary: Marginally each treatment shows a positive effect (feature_014 +6.7pp, feature_001 +5.6pp, feature_021 +5.4pp, all p < 1e-50). However, those marginal effects are pooled across patients with and without the other treatments and may misrepresent the true single-agent benefit (see iterations 8-11).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001, feature_014, feature_021 act additively on objective_response: pairwise interactions are zero on the logit scale.

analyses.code: Logit(resp ~ tx1+tx2+tx1*tx2) for every treatment pair
analyses.result_summary: Strongly refuted. All three pairwise treatment interactions are large and highly significant on the logit scale: feature_014*feature_001 +1.06 (p=7e-103), feature_014*feature_021 +1.05 (p=6e-102), feature_001*feature_021 +0.88 (p=1e-78). The treatments combine super-additively rather than additively.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The three treatments interact in a true three-way fashion: only the simultaneous presence of all three (the triple combination) drives the apparent benefit, while any single agent or pair is essentially inert.
Patients receiving exactly two of the three treatments have a higher response rate than patients receiving none.

analyses.result_summary: In a saturated 3-way logistic model, all single-agent main effects and all 3 pairwise interactions are small and only marginally non-zero (|beta| <= 0.10), but the three-way interaction term feature_001*feature_014*feature_021 is +2.13 (z = 20.4, p = 4e-92). The data are consistent with NO benefit from any subset of one or two treatments, and a large benefit only when all three are given simultaneously.

Refuted. Response rate by number of treatments received: 0 -> 17.2% (n=12533), 1 -> 16.2% (n=22245), 2 -> 16.3% (n=12821), 3 -> 63.3% (n=2401). One- and two-treatment groups are indistinguishable from no treatment; the benefit appears only at three-of-three.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-positive subgroup (feature_001=feature_014=feature_021=1), the response rate is substantially higher than in any other treatment cell.
Within the triple-positive subgroup, response is heterogeneous: at least one binary feature splits this subgroup into a clear high-response and low-response stratum.

analyses.result_summary: Of the eight 0/1 cells, seven have response rate 0.16-0.17 (range 0.157-0.171, all overlap 95% CIs), and one cell -- (1,1,1) -- has response rate 0.633 (n=2401). Triple-positive vs any other cell: chi-square p < 1e-300; risk difference ~ +47pp.

analyses.code: Within triple-pos: chi2 of every binary feature with response
analyses.result_summary: Two binary features dramatically modify response within triple-positive: feature_026 (response 0.184 if =1 vs 0.717 if =0; chi2 p=8e-86) and feature_009 (0.188 vs 0.691; chi2 p=3e-59). No other binary feature comes close (next p ~ 0.14).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_026 is a treatment-effect modifier: it abolishes the triple-treatment benefit. Specifically, in patients with feature_026=1, the response rate does not differ between triple-treated and others.

analyses.code: Within feature_026==1 subgroup: response by triple
analyses.result_summary: Among patients with feature_026=1 (n=7462), triple-positive response rate is 0.184 (n=376) vs 0.169 in non-triple (n=7086); chi-square p=0.50. Triple therapy is statistically indistinguishable from no triple in this stratum. By contrast, feature_026=0 carries triple-effect of +55pp.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_009 is a second, independent treatment-effect modifier: it also abolishes the triple-treatment benefit. In patients with feature_009=1, triple therapy does not increase response.

analyses.result_summary: Among patients with feature_009=1 (n=4985), triple-positive response is 0.188 (n=276) vs 0.170 in non-triple (n=4709); chi-square p=0.41. Treatment effect is again abolished. feature_009 acts as a second, distinct resistance marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_026 and feature_009 are independent of each other (i.e., they identify two different resistance phenotypes rather than measuring the same biology twice).

analyses.result_summary: P(feature_026=1) = 0.149; P(feature_026=1 | feature_009=1) = 0.150 (within triple-positive: 0.149 vs 0.131). Chi-square p ~ 0.95 cohort-wide. The two markers are nearly perfectly independent and likely represent separate resistance mechanisms (or overlapping but biologically distinct events).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_026 and feature_009 are themselves independent of each of the three treatments (i.e., they are baseline patient features, not treatment-related).

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(crosstab(marker, tx)) for each marker x tx pair
analyses.result_summary: feature_026 vs each of feature_001/014/021: chi-square p = 0.73, 0.38, 0.63. feature_009 vs each: p = 0.02, 0.78, 0.12. None are significant after Bonferroni correction. Both markers are pre-treatment patient features, consistent with random assignment of the three treatments.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Combining feature_026 and feature_009: in patients with neither marker (feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0), the triple-treatment response rate is even higher than the marginal triple-positive value of 0.633.

analyses.result_summary: Among feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0 (n=38321), triple-positive (n=1790) response rate is 0.786 vs non-triple (n=36531) 0.165; chi-square p < 1e-300; absolute treatment effect = +62.1pp.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-positive AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0 subgroup, feature_004 (probable performance status, 0=best, 2=worst) shows a residual monotone negative effect on response.

analyses.result_summary: feature_004=0: response 0.859 (n=617); feature_004=1: 0.772 (n=902); feature_004=2: 0.668 (n=271). Logistic coefficient on feature_004 within this subgroup = -0.576, p<0.001. Higher feature_004 reduces but does not abolish the triple-treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-positive AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0 subgroup, the originally important continuous predictors (feature_020, feature_024, feature_022, feature_027, feature_029) retain a residual association with response of the same direction as in the full cohort.

analyses.code: Logit on continuous features within clean triple-positive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within clean triple-positive (n=1790): feature_020 beta -0.054 p<0.001 (still negative), feature_022 beta -0.0038 p=0.08 (trend negative), feature_024 beta -0.0006 p=0.85 (no longer significant), feature_029 beta +0.230 p=0.05 (trend positive), feature_027 beta -0.0056 p=0.33. Most directional patterns persist but only feature_020 (and feature_004) are clearly significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adjusting for feature_004, feature_011 (age), feature_020, feature_022, feature_024, feature_029 in the feature_026=0/feature_009=0 stratum, the triple-treatment indicator retains an effect on the logit scale of similar magnitude (>2).

analyses.code: Logit(resp ~ triple + feature_004 + feature_011 + feature_020 + feature_022 + feature_024 + feature_029) within feat026=0 & feat009=0
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted triple coefficient = +3.00 (OR 20.2; 95% CI 17.9-22.7), z=49.9, p<1e-300. feature_011 (age) is non-significant (beta +0.0004, p=0.75) confirming age does not influence response within the no-resistance stratum. feature_004, feature_020, feature_022, feature_024 each retain independent negative effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Triple treatment effect is further increased among patients with feature_004=0 (the best performance-status-like value) and feature_026=0 and feature_009=0: response rate exceeds 85%.

analyses.result_summary: Among feature_004=0 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0 (n=13448), triple-positive (n=617) response = 0.859 vs non-triple (n=12831) 0.208; risk difference +65.1pp; chi-square p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_026 is biologically a TP53-mutation-like adverse marker: it has a small NEGATIVE main effect on response when no triple therapy is given.

analyses.result_summary: In non-triple patients (n=47599): feature_026=1 response 0.166 (n=7086) vs feature_026=0 response 0.165 (n=40513), p=0.84. The difference is essentially zero -- feature_026 only manifests as a strong adverse modifier under triple therapy. This is consistent with a treatment-resistance / mutational marker that does not affect baseline response biology.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_009 likewise has no main effect on response among non-triple-treated patients and only manifests as a resistance marker when triple therapy is given.

analyses.result_summary: Non-triple patients: feature_009=1 response 0.169 (n=4709) vs feature_009=0 response 0.190 (n=42890), chi2 p=2e-4. There is a small (-2pp) negative main effect, but the effect modification under triple therapy is dramatically larger (-50pp), so feature_009 functions principally as a treatment-effect modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with feature_026=1 OR feature_009=1, NO subgroup of feature values produces a meaningful triple-treatment benefit (i.e., the resistance is essentially complete).

analyses.code: Within feature_026==1 | feature_009==1: chi2 of each feature with response, then combine top splits
analyses.result_summary: In the resistance subgroup (n=11679, of whom 611 received triple), the triple effect on response is +0.018 (chi2 p=0.41). Splits on every other binary or by feature_004 level fail to recover any subgroup with a triple-positive response rate above 25%. The resistance is essentially complete on the average and not rescued by any other measured feature.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive treatment-by-feature interaction screen of feature_001, feature_014, feature_021 with every other feature individually yields no large interaction beyond the triple-positive synergy and the feature_026/feature_009 modifiers.

analyses.code: for tx in [001,014,021]: for f in feats: Logit(resp ~ tx + f + tx*f); rank by interaction p
analyses.result_summary: Beyond the three-way treatment-treatment synergies and feature_026/feature_009, no other interaction reaches p<1e-5 in any of 3 x 34 = 102 tests. The next-largest signals involve feature_020 (p~2e-3 for tx*feature_020 in feature_001 and feature_014), but these effects disappear within the clean subgroup, suggesting they reflect baseline prognostic structure rather than effect modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A continuous-by-treatment heterogeneity test (logistic regression with triple x feature_X interactions) shows feature_024 modifies triple effect, but the modification disappears within the clean subgroup (feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0).

analyses.code: Logit(resp ~ triple + f + triple*f) for each continuous f
analyses.result_summary: Cohort-wide, only feature_024 produces an interaction p<0.01 (triple x feature_024 beta=+0.0080, p=5.6e-4): higher feature_024 weakens triple benefit. Within feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0, the corresponding interaction p=0.85, confirming feature_024 acts marginally only because feature_026/feature_009 confound the cohort-wide test. No other continuous interaction reaches significance in either model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: The triple combination of feature_001, feature_014, and feature_021 (and only the triple combination -- no single agent or pair) dramatically increases objective_response in patients without resistance markers feature_026 and feature_009. The complete predictive subgroup definition is: feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0; in this subgroup response rate is 0.786 (n=1790) vs 0.165 in matched non-triple controls (n=36531). feature_004 (a 3-level performance-status-like variable) is a continuous response modifier within this subgroup but does not gate the treatment effect.

FINAL: feature_026=1 OR feature_009=1 defines a treatment-resistant subgroup in which the triple combination of feature_001+feature_014+feature_021 produces no meaningful improvement in objective_response (response rate 0.185 vs 0.167 baseline, difference not significant).

analyses.result_summary: Final clean subgroup (feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0, n=1790): response rate = 0.7860. Compared with triple-eligible patients receiving fewer than three treatments in the same no-resistance stratum (n=36531, response 0.1653): chi-square p < 1e-300, absolute risk difference +0.621, OR ~ 19.0. Within this subgroup, increasing feature_004 reduces but does not eliminate the response (level 0: 0.859; level 1: 0.772; level 2: 0.668).

Resistance subgroup (feature_026=1 OR feature_009=1, n=11679): triple-positive (n=611) response 0.185 vs non-triple (n=11068) response 0.167; chi-square p=0.20; absolute difference +0.018. Triple therapy delivers no meaningful benefit in this stratum.
